text:
Day three of the Paris Olympics brings one of the stars of Tokyo as Kellie Harrington begins her tournament in the boxing arena.

Harrington is looking to bring some smiles to face for the Irish team after a tricky start that has seen just one fighter progress, Jack Marley.

She takes on Alessia Mesiano just after 2.45pm.

It's also a huge day in the pool with some realistic medal chances.

Mona McSharry was so impressive inreaching the final of the women's 100m breaststroke. She will compete in the final at 8.25pm.

Earlier in the day Daniel Wiffen, Danielle Hill and Ellen Walshe begin their heats in respective events from 10am.

The day starts with the men's hockey team facing Australia at 9am.

Liam Jegou competes in the Canoe Slalom this afternoon.

The women's rugby sevens side also take on Australia at lunchtime.

Badminton star Nhat Nguyen brought great entertainment to viewers in a primetime slot last Saturday night when he opened with a victory. His second group match is against Prince Dahal from Nepal, beginning at 8.10pm at the earliest.

In sailing, Robert Dickson and Sean Waddilove look to improve on their 13th place finish in Japan as they begin the second day in the water.

There is also action in showjumping and rowing on what should be another enjoyable feast of action in Paris.

You can follow the Olympic Games action all day with our live blog with RTÉ Sport and the RTÉ News app, watch live on RTÉ2 and RTÉ Player, and listen to updates on RTÉ Radio 1 and 2fm.

(In order of start time)

0900-1030– Men's Pool B: Ireland v Australia

1000Women’s Lightweight Double Sculls repechage: Margaret Cremen, Aoife Casey

1000-1013Women's 400m individual medley – Ellen Walshe (Heat 2)

1013-1028Women’s 100m backstroke – Danielle Hill (Heat 3)

1028-1108Men’s 800m freestyle – Daniel Wiffen (Heat 4)

1930-1940Women's 400m individual medley – Ellen Walshe (Final if qualifed)

1957-2009Women’s 100m backstroke – Danielle Hill (Semi-final if qualified)

2025-2031Women’s 100m breaststroke – Mona McSharry (Final)

Skiff Races 4-6

1300-1340 + 1352-1432 + 1447-1524:Robert Dickson, Sean Waddilove

Women's Pool Phase – Pool B:

1330Ireland v Australia

2000Quarter-final if qualified

1430-1515Men's C1 – Liam Jegou - Semi-final

1620-1704Final

1446- Women's 60kg – Kellie Harrington v Alessia Mesiano (Italy)

2010onwards - Group P: Nhat Nguyen v Prince Dahal (Nepal)

title: Paris 2024: Irish in action on Day 3 of Olympic Games
description: There's plenty of Irish interest on Day 2 of the Olympic Games. Here's your full schedule for Monday's events.
